name: Spanakopita
recipeInstructions:
1. If using fresh spinach, wash the spinach and remove the rough stems. Place in a large bowl and sprinkle heavily with salt. Rub the salt into the leaves by turning them up, a bunch at a time, and rubbing them between your hands; the volume of spinach will decrease drastically as you do this. Tear the spinach as you rub it. Rinse the salt off thoroughly and dry the spinach, squeezing it in bunches in a towel. (If using frozen spinach, just let it thaw and squeeze out the excess moisture.)
2. Sauté the onion and mushrooms in a little olive oil and butter with garlic and salt and pepper to taste until both onions and mushrooms are tender. Remove from heat.
3. In a large bowl, beat the eggs. Crumble in the feta cheese, then add the grated parmesan, spinach, sautéed onions, and mushrooms. Stir in chopped parsley, oregano, rosemary (if using), additional fresh ground pepper, and a little salt (remembering that the feta is very salty).
